  6. Click on Properties and replace the placeholder values with appropriate values for your setup using the information below:
    • sf.company.id : Replace with placeholder value with your actual SuccessFactors CompanyID. For e.g. DemoCompany in my screenshot.
    • URL : Specify the API URL corresponding to your SuccessFactors instance. For e.g. https://api8.cert.successfactors.com in my setup. Refer to the preparation steps if unsure of what URL to use.
    • sf.user.filter : Update the userName filter to include your own SuccessFactors users. Use or to add additional users who should be replicated into Work Zone when this job is run later.
      Note: The reason for using a filter for specific users is so that we don't replicate the full SuccessFactors user base into Work Zone. This setup will make it easier to test with few users first before rolling out to larger number of users. The userName part of the filter can be removed later if full user synch is required.

       

  1. Click Save.
  2. Click on Outbound Certificates icon and click Download Certificate icon. This will download the certificate into your downloads folder. Note: This certificate will need to be imported into SAP SuccessFactors system in later steps.

     

Configure SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition as a target system in Identity Provisioning

  1. From the menu, access Identity Provisioning >> Target Systems.

     

  2. Under Target Systems click +Add icon.

     

  3. Click Browse to import a pre-defined target system configuration.
  4. Select the WorkZone_Target_ForJoule.json file downloaded earlier and click Open.

     

  5. From the Source System dropdown make sure to select the source system created earlier. For e.g. SuccessFactors - DemoCompany-Joule in my screenshot.

     

  6. Switch to the Properties tab and update the following placeholders with the appropriate values for your system:
  • cflp.providerId: <-- **ID** of the content provider create in Work Zone. **SFSF** in my case -->
  • URL: <--portal-service url from the key file downloaded earlier when we create Work Zone instance in BTP-->
  • OAuth2TokenServiceURL: <--url field from the key file downloaded earlier. Make sure to add /oauth/token to end of the URL-->
  • User: <--clientid from key file downloaded earlier-->
  • Password: <--clientsecret from key file downloaded earlier-->

     

  1. Click Save.

Import the certificate into SAP SuccessFactors

  1. Log into SAP SuccessFactors using an admin user.
  2. Access Security Center from search area.

     

  3. Click X.509 Certificate Mapping tile.
    Note: If the tile is not visible, make sure you have the appropriate permissions assigned to your SuccessFactors admin user. See preparation section.

     

  4. Click the Add button.

     

  5. Specify the following information and Save your configuration:
  • Configuration Name: <--Name of your choice--->
  • Integration Name: Select Identity Provisioning Service from the dropdown.
  • Certificate File: Upload the certificate file downloaded from the SuccessFactors source system we created in earlier steps.
  • Login Name: <--Leave blank-->

     

  1. Confirm the certificate is successfully uploaded in SuccessFactors.

     

 

Add Joule Specific Permission for Users in SAP SuccessFactors

  1. In SFSF instance, navigate to Admin Center-> Manage Permission Groups.

 

  1. Click Create New to create a new group for users who will require access to Joule.
  2. Specify a Group Name and click Pick a Category.

     

  3. Search for the username and select the users who should be having JOULE access. Click Done

     

  4. Click Done again to save your changes for the group.

     

  5. Navigate to Admin Center-> Manage Permission Roles.

     

  6. Click Create New. Specify a Role Name and click Permissions:

     

  7. Under General User Permission >> Access to Joule permission and click Done.
    Note: You may need to start a brand new browser session if the permission is not visible.

     

  8. Under Grand this role to.. Click Add and select the permission group created earlier.

     

     

     

  9. Click Done.

     

  10. Click Done again on the next screen.
  11. Click Save Changes.

     

NOTE: Above steps will allow authorized users to access the Joule functionality and based on user's data access for supported use case, information will be available. Also, this permission is only visible after booster execution in BTP subaccount.

 

Run Refresh Synthetic Group Data Job in SAP SuccessFactors

  1. Login to SuccessFactors Application. 
  2. Navigate to Admin Center-> Scheduled Job Manager.
  3. Under Job Scheduler tab, click on Create Job Request.

     

  4. Enter the following information:
    • Job Name
    • Job Type: Refresh Synthetic Group Data
    • Job Parameters: Common Data Model Content Deep-Link Access User Group
    • Occurrence: Recurring
    • Recurrence Pattern: Daily
    • Start Date: Use current date and set to execute in few minutes
    • End Date: Time frame of your choice

 

 

 

  1. Click Submit.

Note: This job must be successfully executed once before proceeding to the next step. Set the start time of job to execute so that it executes in the next few minutes.
Note: This job execution would make sure all the users who has been assigned deep link specific access are refreshed and synced correctly during Identity Provisioning sync to SAP Build Work Zone application.

 

Provision Users and Groups from SAP SuccessFactors to Work Zone

  1. Access the administration console of SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant using one of the URL formats below: 
    • https://your-ias-tenant.accounts.ondemand.com/admin
    • https://your-ias-tenant.accounts.cloud.sap/admin
      Note: Substitute your-ias-tenant with your actual tenant's name.
  2. Authenticate using an administrator user.
  3. From the menu, access Identity Provisioning >> Source Systems.
  4. Under Source Systems select the source system we created earlier for Joule integration and click Job.

     

  5. Click Run Now to run the Read job.

     

  6. From the menu, access Identity Provisioning >> Provisioning Logs.

     

  7. Confirm the job executes successfully.
    Note: It may take few minutes for the job to appear in this interface. The job execution time will also vary depending upon number of users being read. It may take up to 15 minutes to provision few users and groups that meet the filter criteria specified in the job properties. For full user synch it can take several hours.

     

  8. Click the job to view the Job Execution Details and confirm users and groups that meet the filter criteria are created in Work Zone application.

     

Validate User Provisioning in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Note: In order to run through these steps, you will need to ensure Launchpad_Admin role is assigned to the user accessing Work Zone. 

  1. Access BTP Cockpit URL.
  2. Select the BTP Global Account that has the Joule entitlements and click Continue.
  3. Select the BTP Subaccount where Joule was setup.
  4. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select Instances and Subscriptions*. Click SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition to launch the application.
  5. If prompted to choose the authentication provider, click Default Identity Provider or your SAP Cloud Identity Authentication Service URL.
  6. Under Channel Manager click the Update Content icon.

     

  7. Click Report to see the content was successfully created for the SuccessFactors content provider.

     

  8. Click Settings >> Identity Provisioning. Under Explore Role Assignments section, search for user using their email and click View Assigned Roles.
    Note: The user should have some roles assigned. The Provider id should match what we setup in Work Zone specifically for Joule integration.

     

Test Joule in SAP SuccessFactors

NOTE: To test Employee Central specific use cases you must have Employee Central Quick Links also enabled. See the pre-requisites section for more information on how to enable Employe Central Quick Links. The full list of supported use cases for Joule are accessible from here. 

  1. Login to SuccessFactors application with user who has been granted JOULE access.
  2. Click on JOULE icon visible on home page.

     

  3. This would launch JOULE and you are ready to test the supported use cases available for SuccessFactors.

     

  4. Type View Direct Manager.

     

  5. Enter Mine to view your managers details.

     

  6. Click on this Navigation link to view details of your manager profile.

     

  7. Confirm that you can successfully navigate to page where your managers profile is visilbe.
  8. Test additional use cases as described in the link above.

 

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: How to delete amended record of timesheet for particular week into SuccessFactors?
entry.content:
I will not show how employee submit the timesheet and how approval process happens.

I will show the procedure directly, If employee tries to amend the approved timesheet then the original timesheet will be overridden and new timesheet record will be created.

In backend admin can only access these records using below navigation:

Manage data-> Employee timesheet

Example:

Employee has submitted the timesheet and it was approved.

First time System will generate one external code for a timesheet, if it’s amended by employee then it’s replaced with new external code

 

For example:

Employee had amended the approved timesheet and submitted for approval again:

Now the timesheet new external code generated by system and old record will be stored in replaces External code shown in below image.

In the backend the new External code will show as approved:

Replaced external code is old one and it will show as cancelled into backend, this replaced external code should be deleted from the system to avoid issues.

Replaces- 3fe750fcd3de4680a9c87dab52477b1e

To delete the replaces external code click on "permanently Delete entry" option.

Click on Yes to continue

After deletion of replaced external code system will show only one record/external code into trace for the particular week timesheet:

Note: 

If you want to do bulk deletion they you need to download the "employee timesheet" data via "Import and Export data" tool

then filter the cancelled external code and upload with "delete" operation.

Conclusion:

This type of issue comes when the employees are allowed to amend the timesheet after approval.

We should delete the replaced one and new one will reside into system with actual hours.

The deletion will avoid issue of multiple records in backend and we can keep only actual records for particular week.

entry.title: Recurring deduction future timeslice records mass deletion
entry.content:
Requirement:

Delete the future time slice data or pay components from recurring deductions portlet, we can use the below import process so that the a particular time slice record or a particular pay component will be removed from employees recurring deductions data.

Below sample employee has multiple records in recurring deductions, we must remove all pay component deductions which start from Jan.2026 for all employees.

Possible Solution:

Mass deletion of the required time slice and removing all deductions for employees can be done with the import.

Export data of existing employees recurring deductions with “Import and export data” with Generic object as “Recurring deduction” with including dependencies “Yes”.

  • To remove the deduction, pay components from future dated records –
  1. Open the file “Recurring Deduction-Recurring Items” to remove the Jan.2026 pay component records for the appropriate employees.
  2. Insert “DELIMIT” operation for the deductions which are not required as shown in below picture.
  3. Import the CSV file for generic object “Recurring Deduction-Recurring Items”.

 

  • To remove the Jan.2026 time slice for all the deductions available for user-

Open the file “Recurring Deduction.csv” this file will have all the employee recurring deduction records with employee number and the effective date of the deduction.

  1. Open the file “Recurring Deduction.csv” to remove the Jan.2026 time slice.
  2. Remove the timeslice records not required( as highlighted in below picture) or Insert “DELETE” operation for the deductions which are not required
  3. Import the CSV file for generic object “Recurring Deduction”.

Below screenshot shows the export file of recurringdeduction.csv

Below picture shows the import file which has the user records after removing the not required time slice.

For the below sample employee, Jan.2026 time slice is now removed from the employee recurring deductions portlet after the successful import.

This is how to delete Recurring Deductions from employee profile. Hope this blog is helpful.

entry.title: Benefits auto enrolment for New Hires and auto delimitation for Terminations
entry.content:
Requirement:

All new starters to be enrolled in mandatory Global benefits of SuccessFactors like life insurance and all terminated employees to be auto unenrolled or delimited from all the enrolled benefits.

Solution:

This is possible by using simple rules through below steps and similarly for all terminations or retirements based on the events recorded in employee data.

Once this configuration is complete you could see that all the employees benefits deductions are automatically updated without any manual work.

 

  • As a first step, create the business rule which will trigger intelligent service center events once the employee is hired or terminated and assign the rules as post save event type to the Business configuration Job information.

 

 

 

  • next step is to  setup Intelligent service Center, it is like middleware which will sync the events data once we create the events. Standard events hire, rehire and termination are already created by SAP and use the same flows, for any validations or any approvals to trigger we can add rules. The below is the standard flow provided by SAP.

 

 

  • events received from employee data will be processed further by creating “Benefit Event Determination” for required events with list of legal entities.

 

 

 

 

  • create benefit life events and update life event configuration details which will create auto enrollment of benefits based on the rules defined in this configuration.

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Sample effective date autoenrollment business rule for creating autoenrollment or auto delimitation with the start of the next month in employee profile with the rule scenario as “Configure Benefit enrollment effective from rule via Benefit work/life event configuration”.

 

Termination rule

 

 

Testing employees for autoenrollment for new hire and auto optout for Termination:

 

 

 

 

Employee Terminated on 13th Nov.2024 and the employee was opted out from all benefits from 01st of December.

This blog is created in detail to successfully enroll or un enroll employee into benefits based on master data actions. Hope this blog is helpful.

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Version Management in SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
entry.content:
Version Management within SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) for Planning is an area that needs to be organized and pristine.  This blog explores the differences between Private and Public versions and how to manage versions effectively using examples from best-practice business content, such as the Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA.

Understanding Private and Public Versions in SAP Analytics Cloud

Private Versions and Public Versions serve distinct purposes in SAP Analytics Cloud:

  • Private Versions are individual workspaces where planners can experiment with scenarios and updates without affecting others. Private Versions allow users to test different planning approaches in isolation.

  • Public Versions are shared, organization-wide Versions that serve as the official source of truth for FP&A data. When a planner inputs data into a Public Version, their inputs are captured in a Private Version. The planner must then actively choose to "publish" their changes so that they appear in the Public Version.

Establish a Single Planning Version

The process within the Rapid FP&A Suite uses a single, write-enabled, public PLAN version—the Working Plan—for all active planning activities. This approach minimizes confusion by avoiding multiple editable Plan Versions simultaneously. Multiple editable Versions (e.g., Plan_1, Plan_2) upfront, can:

  • Confuse planners about where to submit their data.

  • Require constant updates to input forms and associated security settings for each Version.

  • Introduce excessive variability into workflows, leading to errors.

To simplify the process, the SAP consultants behind Rapid FP&A suggest:

  • Focus on a Single PLAN Version: Using a single PLAN Version ensures planners always know where to input their data. This reduces confusion and eliminates the administrative burden of maintaining multiple editable Versions.

  • Pre-Configured Input Forms in Rapid FP&A: Input forms in the Rapid FP&A Suite are pre-configured to filter on the PLAN Version, ensuring planners work in the "Working Plan" Version.

  • Leverage Data Actions: The Rapid FP&A Suite includes pre-configured Data Actions that allow you to copy any PLAN data into finalized, version-specific names like Target2025 or Budget2024. Once copied, these versions become read-only and are naturally referred to by their specific names (e.g., Forecast2025) for clarity and consistency.

Why does this approach work? It focuses planners on completing one version of the Plan before moving to another, avoiding the complexity and errors of managing multiple editable versions simultaneously.

Managing Data Within Private Versions

Private Versions offer a flexible environment to refine and validate planning scenarios before publishing to Public Versions. Here are some best practices:

How to Use Private Versions Effectively

Planners must flow their plan inputs through Private Versions, regardless of how Public Versions are managed, so educating Planners on how to use Private Versions optimally is crucial. Proper use of Private Versions ensures accurate updates to the plan and provides  flexibility:

  • Experiment and Refine: Use Private Versions to model scenarios, refine your inputs, and validate changes without impacting others.

  • Publish with Purpose: Once all updates are finalized, publish the changes to the Public Version to make them available for organizational use. Under the Rapid FP&A Suite, published updates flow into the PLAN Version (Working Plan), which admins can later archive into specific Version names (e.g., Budget2026) once all plans are collected.

  • Utilize Tools for Confidence: Features like the “Revert” function and “Version History Panel” provide a safety net to undo mistakes during refinement of your Private version. Remember: Reverting is unavailable after publishing it in the Public Version.

Tip: The Rapid FP&A Suite includes pre-configured input forms and sample data, allowing you to practice updating and publishing Private Versions in a "practice" environment.

Best Practices for Managing Public Versions

Creating multiple specific Version names (e.g., Budget2026, Forecast2025) at the start of every planning cycle can be problematic. This approach requires updating all input forms and reports to align with each new Version name, creating inefficiencies and potential confusion. Additionally, frequent changes to Version names can leave users unsure where to input their plan data, causing unnecessary delays and errors.

Use Categories to Organize Your Versions

You can use the category property to organize your versions by type. Categories help distinguish versions and streamline management. The following categories can help organize versions:

  • Actual, Budget, Plan, Forecast, Rolling Forecast

A model can have one public Actuals version, typically updated via import from a data source like S/4HANA. However, private copies of this version can be created for analysis or testing. Categories like Forecast allow you to create multiple forecast versions to explore optimistic, baseline, or worst-case outcomes.

Under Best Practices in the Rapid FP&A Suite, planners will focus on one scenario at a time (e.g., baseline) within the PLAN Version. Once the scenario is finalized, it can be archived under a version-specific name such as Baseline2025 or Optimistic2025, and then the planner can move on to the following scenario. This approach ensures planners complete one scenario before moving to another, avoiding confusion and streamlining the process.

Hold Actual Data in a Read-Only Version

  • Store finalized Actual data in a read-only ACTUAL Version. This ensures the integrity of historical data and provides a clear baseline for variance analysis.

Archive Specific Versions for Reporting

  • Instead of constantly updating input forms and security for new write-enabled Versions, take snapshots of the working PLAN Version at key points in the planning cycle. Archive these snapshots into finalized, read-only Versions (e.g., Budget2026, Forecast2025) using pre-built Data Actions from the Rapid FP&A Suite.

  • This approach preserves the integrity of snapshot data while avoiding the repetitive task of reconfiguring templates and security for every user or group needing write access to a plan version.

How Rapid FP&A Suite Supports Version Management

The Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA is a valuable toolset for:

  1. Testing and Experimentation

    • Use sample data to explore how updates to Private Versions flow into Public Versions.

  2. Streamlined Workflows

    • Discover how we maintain a single write-enabled PLAN Version for all planning activities.

    • See how admins can create finalized, locked-down Versions efficiently by copying a version from PLAN

  3. Reducing Administrative Overhead

    • You will see how pre-configured input forms align with the active working PLAN Version.

    • With only one active PLAN version all the time, you simplify security and configuration management

  4. Preserving Data Integrity

    • Finalized Versions like Budget2026 or Forecast2025 are clearly labeled and locked for accurate reporting.

    • Any other interim snapshots of PLAN can be archived for reviews or status meetings.

Benefits for FP&A Teams?

  • Planners: Clarity on where to input data with a single active Version.

  • Admins: Efficiency in managing Versions with reduced maintenance effort.

  • Stakeholders: Reliable, finalized Versions ready for variance analysis and decision-making.

Getting Started

Ready to unlock the full potential of Version Management? Leverage the Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA as a toolset to test and refine your planning processes. Explore its pre-configured content, including input forms, data actions, and sample scenarios, to simplify and standardize your workflows. The best part? You can download the suite in minutes and review these processes using sample data—no integration is required during your discovery phase.

How do you Download the free Rapid FP&A Suite?
Downloading the Rapid FP&A Suite is straightforward and hassle-free.
Follow these steps to get started:
1. Log in to your SAP Analytics Cloud account.   Please don't use a production tenant.
2. Navigate to the "Content Network" on the left-hand side panel of SAC
3. Select "Business Content"
4. Search for "Rapid" in the upper right-hand 'Search' box - you should see Rapid Finance, Workforce, Sales
4. Follow the instructions to complete the download and installation of each of the Rapid modules (Rapid Finance, Rapid Sales, Rapid Workforce).

Let’s make planning Rapid, Simple, and Standard with SAP Analytics Cloud!

entry.title: One month Pension Benefit, available always for enrolment for all including enrolled employees
entry.content:
Requirement:

Unique Pension Benefit that will always be available for enrolment including benefit enrolled employees for next month enrolment, employee to add the contribution only once for each month and one time deduction happens in compensation.

Possible Solution:

  • Create new benefit as Pension benefit type with all mandatory fields

  • Create Pension fund with only employee pay component, as shown in below picture and assign it to new 1 month pension benefit.

  • Create employee contribution business rule which will default minimum employee contribution amount by using rule scenario “Determine Contribution Amount for Pension/Retirement Savings Plan” and assign it to the employee pension contribution rule

  • Create new benefit schedule, monthly schedule periods with enrollment open, close dates and enrolment valid dates as shown in below picture and assign the “1Month schedule” benefit schedule to benefit.

This setup completes required configuration and its now time for testing.

Testing:

Benefit behaves as per the requirement:  

  • Benefit enrolment on 24th Jan will auto display effective from following month i.e. feb.2025:

     

  • Benefit enrolment automatically inactivates on following month of enrolment (i.e. inactivates enrolment from March.2025):

  • 1 time pension contribution component is removed automatically from March.2025: 

  • Always available for next month enrolment, 1time pension is not listed as active enrolment as of 5th feb.2025:

entry.title: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
Accelerating innovation while future-proofing your business

(Note: This is a fast-moving project so please bookmark this blog as we will keep updating the information as it becomes available)

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) is the gold standard for enterprise and global payroll with localization in 57 countries (52 by SAP, 5 by partners) and live productive usage in 84 (and counting). With more than 2000 companies around the globe, it is delivering more than 200 million pay statements per year.

ECP is SAP’s go-to, long-term payroll solution fueled by significant ongoing investment. Over last few months, we have embarked on a journey to revamp your favorite payroll product.  We are converting ECP to a modern technology foundation which will unlock new capabilities and elevate its performance for decades to come. We are working hard so these innovations reach our global customers quickly

Why is SAP embarking on this initiative?

The current technologies used for ECP are going out of maintenance by end of 2027. This has opened an opportunity for us to leverage the powerful capabilities of the new technology foundation as well as new tools to improve on our service delivery while enabling you to future-proof your business for years to come.

What are the benefits to our customers?

Among the key capabilities and innovations our customers will be able to experience through this technology modernization are:

  • Rollout of new features to meet customer demand
    • Position Budgeting Control (PBC) for regulated industries (e.g. public sector, healthcare, higher education)
    • Business AI capabilities to drive HR operations efficiency (e.g. Explain my pay statement)
    • Updated HORIZON UI for a unified user experience (consistent with the rest of SAP SuccessFactors HCM)
    • Enhanced Payroll Control Center capabilities to better manage payroll activities

  • Operational  enhancements
    • High availability set-up for production systems
    • Ability to get enhanced encryption (planned)

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

  1. Why is SAP doing this?
    • The current technologies used for ECP are going out of maintenance by end of 2027 which has opened an opportunity for us to leverage the powerful capabilities of the SAP HANA database and associated technologies.

  2. What are the current technologies used for ECP? What modern technology foundation are you moving to?
    • Current technologies: SAP ECC 6.08, SAP NetWeaver 7.40 and SAP MaxDB database
    • Modern technologies: SAP S/4HANA 2023, ABAP Platform 2023, SAP HANA database

  3. When will the modernized ECP available for me? I need to know when to request budget, and resources for my own planning
    • New Customers: At the time of publication of this blog (Jan 31, 2025), all our new customers in USA, Australia and Europe are being provisioned EC Payroll on modern technology foundation. Rest of the world will follow over coming months.
    • Existing Customers: Conversion planning is in full swing. It will be a phased approach which will start in the second quarter of 2025 and will continue throughout 2027. We will reach out to customers and partners on the details of the conversion planning.

  4. Where can I find documentation how to use the new technology foundation?
    • The standard ECP documentation has been enhanced with chapters targeting the new technology foundation and is located here

  5. How long will the conversion last, when is the latest date that I will be converted?
    • We will start in second quarter 2025 with pilot customers and targeting to complete it by the end of 2027

  6. How should we plan for the conversion to modernized SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll?
    • We will publish the preliminary version of a detailed conversion guide by End-March.

  7. Will the Business AI capabilities like Explain my pay statement functionality be offered only on the modernized ECP or will it be available for current ECP version also?
    • All the new innovations and enhancements will be available ONLY on ECP built on the new technology foundation.

  8. Will the Business AI capabilities like Explain my pay statement functionality be offered on Private Cloud Edition (PCE)?
    • No. They will ONLY be offered on SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll on the modern technology foundation

  9. Who do I contact for questions?
    • Please visit this blog as this will be kept updated as more and more details become available. In the meantime, please contact your SAP account executive or partner for any questions.

entry.title: Using Matches ( ) and Replace Characters
entry.content:
Good day everyone!

Been a while since I did one of these, but here goes...  Today, I want to discuss using the match and replace options in a business rule, to help eliminate failures of especially CSV output files used for integrations.

Why might we want to prevent users from entering a comma at source, rather than changing the delimiter in a CSV output to a PIPE for instance?  Well, many standard integrations do not allow you to change the delimiter of choice and uses a comma to delimit columns by default.  In today's example, we will be looking at preventing position titles to be entered using a comma, but this logic can be expanded to HRIS elements like the Personal Information as well if needed.

Scenario

To start off, let me sketch the scenario.  I have already mentioned, we will be looking at adding these business rules to the Position object definition.  In addition, my requirement is to first raise a warning message, to allow an HR Admin the opportunity to use a different separator.  If they ignore the warning, I will then automatically replace the comma with a semi-colon.

Start off by adding a Save Rule from the Position Object Definition (or Configure Business Rules and adding it after).

I will be adding two business rules, but you can use only 1 if you do not allow users to ignore a warning message, i.e. if a comma is found in the position title, it cannot be saved until the comma is removed.  

Message Definition

Prior to creating my business rules, I will also navigate to Manage Data and create a new message definition that I will use as my warning pop-up.

Rules for MDF Based Objects

Since I will be adding these as Save Rules, I will select the Purpose as Evaluate, along with the Base Object as Position.

Business Rule to raise a warning

Since my instance also uses Spanish and French (languages using special characters often, my business rule cannot simply have a regular expression to allow only the use of alphabet characters and numbers as per KBA 2426526.

As such, my regular expression checks for a match with a comma, and raises the previously created message, with a Warning severity:

  • The first ^ asserts that we're at the beginning of the string  
  • Next [^,] is a character class which means "Any character except ,"
  • + next to something means "Match this one or more times"
  • Finally, $ asserts that we're now at the end of the string

Also remember to include additional Else If conditions for each activated language pack, if needed.

Result

After adding this rule as a Save Rule in Position Object Definition and now entering a comma in a position title (either creating a new position or updating an existing position's title, the warning message will be raised when attempting to save.

 

Now the HR Admin (or responsible person) can click Cancel to update the label using a different delimiter, or No to discard the changes.  But what if they choose to proceed anyway?  Well, then I will just replace it!

Business Rule to replace comma

The business rule is almost identical, but instead of raising a warning message, it will replace the comma.

The replacement character is optional.  If you prefer to simply strip the comma out of the title, leave this blank.

Result

If the user decided to click Yes instead on the warning message (and after the above rule was also added as a Save Rule in Position Object Definition), the character is automatically replaced:

 

Now those pesky commas will no longer add additional commas in CSV outputs and potentially causing some integrations to fail.

Do give some kudos if this blog was helpful. 

Have a good one!

Eugene

entry.title: SAP BTP Extension Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM- Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management
entry.content:
SAP BTP Extension Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management.

 

  1. Current Challenge   :

    When employees are terminated in SAP SuccessFactors, their records are typically inactivated, and they lose access to the system. If the terminated (or retired) employee needs to access or update their personal information, they must create a support ticket and go through manual processes involving HR or IT staff. This process can be time-consuming, prone to delays, and dependent on human intervention, making it less efficient and not very user-friendly.

How Tech Envo Solved this challenge :

Tech Envo built a SAP BTP Extension to solve this problem. This extension Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management Extension will address these challenges.

 
 
  1. Self-Service Access: It would allow retired or terminated employees to retain limited, secure access to SAP SuccessFactors even after their employment ends. This way, they can view and update their information without needing to go through HR or IT teams. This is achieved through maintaining the terminated employee record as a public profile in IAS, allowing the user to get authenticated to the system but have access to only specific information. 
  2. Automated Updates: Instead of raising a ticket, retired employees could directly update details such as bank account information, tax details, or contact information in real time. This automation would reduce the administrative burden on HR teams and streamline the process for former employees.
  3. Enhanced Security: The solution could ensure that only the necessary data is accessible to retired employees, maintaining confidentiality and security while allowing them to manage essential information like pension details, payment preferences, and tax forms.
  4. Improved User Experience: With a user-friendly interface designed for retirees, the process would be made faster, more transparent, and less dependent on support staff intervention.


    Organizations can increase efficiency, reduce support costs, and improve overall employee satisfaction, even after termination by automating and streamlining this process. Would you like to explore how this solution could be technically implemented or what features could be included?

    3) Technology Stack and Architecture Design of the Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management SAP BTP Extension Solution

    The key components of this technology stack are:

    • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): The backbone of our solution, enabling secure and scalable cloud-based operations.
    • SAP SuccessFactors HCM: Integrates employee data management processes, providing a comprehensive HCM solution.
    • SAP WorkZone: Allows easy access and management of applications, enhancing user experience for retirees.
    • SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS): Ensures secure and streamlined user authentication, enhancing security for retired employees.
    • SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Model: Aids in building and deploying scalable and efficient back-end services.
    • Node.js: Utilized for its efficiency and scalability in handling back-end processes.

  The architecture of the Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management Extension Solution for SAP SuccessFactors is designed for efficiency and flexibility. The user interface, crafted with SAP UI5, is both responsive and user-friendly, ensuring retirees can easily navigate the system. At its core, the backend leverages the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model and Node.js, enabling robust data processing and seamless integration with SAP SuccessFactors through a secure BTP destination. This architecture allows the solution to function as a standalone application within SAP Build WorkZone or be embedded into an existing SAP SuccessFactors portlet, delivering a versatile and scalable platform for managing retired employees' data. Hosted on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), the solution ensures data integrity and continuous accessibility, empowering organizations to provide retirees with an intuitive and secure self-service experience.

4) What is the Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management SAP BTP Extension Solution?

TechEnvo's SAP SuccessFactors Pensioner & Alumni Data Management Solution for effortless personal and payment detail access, which empowers organizations to manage retiree and alumni employee data seamlessly. Built on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), this advanced solution simplifies the process for retired employees to access and update their personal and payment details. By leveraging a user-friendly interface and robust integration capabilities, the solution enhances data accuracy, and reduces manual effort, ensuring a superior user experience and improved employee satisfaction. This blog demonstrates how the solution expertly overcomes the key challenges of managing retiree data while fostering stronger connections with alumni employees.

Key Features:

1. Allows Organizations to provide an interface to retired alumni employees.

     -> The application allows retired or alumni employees to access and update their personal information and payment details from SAP Success Factors

    -> By providing a streamlined interface, organizations can ensure that retired employees have complete control over their data.

2.Ability to create, modify, or delete their contact and address information.
     ->The application provides the option to create, update, and delete contact information like phone numbers, country codes, and address information such as city, state, and country.

3.Allows to create, modify, or delete their payment details.
     ->The application allows the creation, update, and deletion of related details like Pay Type, Account Number, Bank Country, Currency, Amount, Bank, IBAN, etc.,

 

 The Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management Extension Solution is an advanced application that provides retired employees a seamless way to access and update their personal and payment details stored in SAP SuccessFactors. It focuses on enhancing user experience and ensuring that retirees have complete control over their data. This solution simplifies processes like modifying contact information or updating payment preferences and ensures that these actions are carried out quickly, securely, and efficiently, leading to improved overall satisfaction for retired employees. Organizations benefit from this tool by maintaining strong connections with their alumni while streamlining administrative processes.

  

 

4) How the TechEnvo's Pensioner & Alumni Solution Benefits Organizations.

  1. Intuitive User Interface for Pensioners - Empowering Self-Service Data Management: The solution utilizes SAP UI5 within SAP BTP to provide a secure and intuitive interface, enabling retirees to manage personal data and payment preferences independently without requiring HR or IT intervention.

    2.Self-Service Data Management for Retirees: Built on SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP), this solution offers retirees the ability to securely modify and delete personal data, leveraging Fiori’s seamless interface, with built-in validation and audit tracking to ensure data consistency and security.

     
    3.Streamlined Payment Information Management: Through SAP BTP’s integration capabilities, retirees can manage payment preferences in real-time, with automated validation and secure data transfers to backend systems like SAP SuccessFactors, ensuring timely, accurate pension payments.

     
    4.Flexible Access Options for Retired Employees: The solution provides flexible access via a standalone SAP Build WorkZone app or as a portlet within SAP SuccessFactors, ensuring seamless integration without disrupting existing systems.
    5.Robust Technology Backbone: Leveraging SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) and Node.js for backend processes, along with SAP UI5 Fiori Elements for a modern, responsive interface, the solution ensures scalability, high performance, and secure data integration with SAP SuccessFactors.

    The Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management Extension Solution provides retirees with secure, self-service access to manage personal and payment data, ensuring seamless integration, real-time updates, and enhanced security. A BTP destination is created to securely connect the application to the SAP SuccessFactors instance of the organization, ensuring data integrity and smooth data flow.

      

     

    5) Why Opt for the Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management Extension Solution

    The Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management Extension Solution leverages the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to provide a robust and secure infrastructure for managing retiree data. By utilizing SAP UI5, the solution offers an intuitive interface, enabling retirees to independently manage their personal details and payment preferences. Built on SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP), the solution ensures seamless integration with SAP SuccessFactors for consistent data synchronization and real-time updates. Security protocols such as encrypted data transfers and compliance with organizational policies ensure the safe handling of sensitive retiree information. This flexible and scalable solution not only reduces HR workload but also provides retirees with secure, easy access to their data while aligning with the organization’s digital transformation goals.

     

    6) Get Started with the Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management Extension Solution

    The Pensioner and Alumni Employee Data Management Extension Solution is now available to integrate with your organization, enabling retirees to manage their data independently. With a user-friendly platform powered by SAP BTP, it simplifies data management and enhances engagement with retired employees. This solution seamlessly integrates with SAP SuccessFactors, ensuring consistent and secure data management. Contact us today to learn how this solution can transform your retirees’ experience and benefit your organization.

entry.title: How to publish data from Comp info to Job info?
entry.content:
Scenario: There is a field Sub Role Band on the job info. An employee Sub Role Band changes during the compensation cycle. How can we publish this new value on the job info?

Resolution: It can be done by making a table report of final value after the compensation cycle and this report needs to be hosted on SFTP server and from there the value will be pulled with the help of integration on the job info field.

Following is the detailed reproduction of issue as well as the solution.

Employee’s Current Sub Role Band:

Change of Sub Role Band during ongoing compensation cycle. Screenshot of the final worksheet:

Now, we have to make a table report of the value (Final Pseudo Role Band) that need to be pushed from compensation to job info.

Steps to be followed for making a report:

Go to Report Centre. Click on New > Table Report > Single Domain > Compensation Planning.

Following is the screenshot of the report. Here for a user both the current and final values have been taken.

Steps For Scheduling a Report:

  • Click on three dots in the right side of the corresponding report.

  • Click on New Schedule. We get four sections to fill:
  • Schedule Definition
  • Filter
  • Destination
  • Schedule Occurrence

  • Give the name of the schedule. Select the file format. Here it is selected as csv. Choose the notification settings if required.
  • Click on step 2. There is nothing to choose at this step. Click on Step 3.
  • Here we give details about the SFTP server where this report file is going to be hosted.

 

  • Select FTP as the method. Then click on the checkbox saying SF hosted SFTP server and provide the credentials. Now provide the file path that is in which folder of SFTP we want to push the report.
  • Here the folder is /incoming. Also provide the file name containing the format as selected during Schedule definition. If we want to include the date format in our file name then select from the format as provided in the dropdown. Though it is not mandatory. After that click on Step 4.

  • Select the frequency of our schedule. Here it is selected as once as the final value is captured after the completion of compensation cycle. Then we need to select the date and time of our first occurrence.

  • After the successful completion of scheduling, the report will appear in the destination folder of SFTP server:

  • Next step is to create an integration to pull this report. Following are the steps:

Search Integration Center > Click on Create > Click on More Integration Types > Choose Integration Type

 

 

Select the options as below:

  • Trigger Type: Scheduled
  • Source Type: SFTP as our file is on SFTP
  • Destination Type: SuccessFactors (Value needs to be reflected on jobInfo portlet)
  • Format: CSV

Click on Create. Then search for job information in the search bar as we need to map the fields on the job information with our report fields. Next click on select.

 

  • In the options setting, select the integration name and write the description if needed. Then click on Next and move to the configure fields section.
  • Here we need to first upload a sample report file in the Sample Data View.

  • Click on Field Mapping View. Apart from mapping our destination field, we need to map certain mandatory fields (User Id, Start Date and Sequence Number).

  • We have to drag and drop User ID from source settings on to the User ID in destination fields to map them.
  • Similarly Final Pseudo Role Band is mapped.
  • Next, click on the Field Detail View and select the default value of sequence number.

  • Now select the start date of the integration. Here it is selected as Today. Else we can set calculation when we want the integration to start.

  • Select “Today” from the dropdown in front of the field value. Click ok.

  • After all the fields are mapped click on Next and configure the Source Settings.

  • File Server Settings to be configured as follows:
  • Enter the SFTP host address.
  • 22 is the default SFTP port.
  • In the authentication type select Basic Authentication.
  • Enter the SFTP credentials.
  • File Name and Folder Settings to be configured as follows:
  • Enter the name of the file (Hosted on SFTP server through Reporting Center) without file format.
  • Date suffix format to be selected if the file name contains the date.
  • Select the file extension as selected while scheduling the report.
  • Select the source folder of the SFTP.
  • Click on Next and set the schedule of the integration.

 

  • Email id can be mentioned if we want a notification for failed integrations. Then click on Next.

  • We need to click on Save once all the settings are configured. Then we can click on Run Now if we want to run the integrations or we can click on Set Schedule if we want the integration to run on our predefined scheduled date and time.
  • After that we can check the status of our integration from Last run time or by following the below steps:
  • Search Integration Center > Monitor Integration > Select the integration to see the details or errors if any.
  • Once the integration runs successfully, we need to check the value of our required field (Sub Role Band) in jobinfo.
  • Attached is the screenshot of jobinfo history notifying the change in value of Sub Role Band.

 

Regards,

Farhan Danish
